title: larix decidua var. russica
intro: Larix decidua var. russica, also known as the Russian larch, is a variant of the European larch tree (Larix decidua) native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The health benefits of Larix decidua var. russica include reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the bark is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
